purpose: Flash-shape grouped-BMM floor for routed MoE math at B=1, S=4096 on one MI350.

net_read.interpretation: Materializing the wgrad LHS transposes improves the three wgrad BMMs, but paying the copies each iteration is a net slowdown. This layout is only worth promoting if a fused upstream backward path produces or reuses the contiguous transposed layout without a standalone copy.

decision.read: At the Flash shape, even balanced routing gives only 96 rows per expert. The core grouped BMM contractions run at roughly 296-500 TFLOPs median, far below the one-expert large-M Flash GEMM probe around 1.1-1.3 PFLOPs. The measured gap is therefore small-M grouped-GEMM efficiency, not mainly dispatch/combine glue.
next_action: Prototype or test CK/CK-Tile, AITER, or TileLang compact-native grouped training GEMM for 256 experts with about 96 rows/expert.
Prioritize backward dgrad/xgrad plus wgrad bundling, since actual-layout wgrad alone costs about 3.93 ms and dgrad/xgrad about 2.74 ms in isolated BMM floor.
Avoid standalone transpose-copy optimization unless the layout can be made free by fusion with SwiGLU derivative or saved activation layout.
